pages.title: opensip-tools Architecture
pages.purpose: Teaching path through opensip-tools — what it is, how it thinks, how it runs — plus a lookup-shaped reference catalog.

pages.title: Quick start
pages.purpose: Four commands from zero to a passing fitness run. Hands-on before the conceptual material.

pages.title: What is opensip-tools
pages.purpose: The product entry point — what problem opensip-tools solves, what it actually does, the philosophy, and what it deliberately is not.

pages.title: Vocabulary
pages.purpose: The terms used everywhere in opensip-tools. Read this once before going deeper.

pages.title: System context
pages.purpose: Where opensip-tools sits between you, your codebase, CI, and OpenSIP Cloud — and what it touches on disk.

pages.title: The fitness loop
pages.purpose: One check, end to end. Definition → loading → recipe selection → scope resolution → execution → signal → render → exit. The spine of the doc set.

pages.title: The tool-plugin model
pages.purpose: How the CLI doesn't know what `fit` does. The Tool contract, the registry, the dispatcher, and what it takes to add a third tool.

pages.title: Layered package graph
pages.purpose: The 18-package monorepo, the five-layer dependency rule, why dependency-cruiser exists, and the trade-offs.

pages.title: Contract surfaces
pages.purpose: The system's public edges. Every contract opensip-tools makes with the outside world, and what changing each one would cost.

pages.title: Recipes and checks
pages.purpose: What a recipe is, what a check is, and how they compose. The two primary author-facing primitives in fit.

pages.title: Targets and scope
pages.purpose: How the framework decides which files a check runs against. Targets, scope, glob expansion, and the marketplace shape.

pages.title: Ignore directives
pages.purpose: Inline source-level suppression — how `@fitness-ignore-next-line` and `@fitness-ignore-file` work, when to use them, and where they fit in the run.

pages.title: Output, gate, SARIF
pages.purpose: What happens to the violations a check produces — render layer, JSON output, SARIF, the gate, cloud reporting.

pages.title: Scenarios and recipes (sim)
pages.purpose: What a sim scenario is, the four kinds, and how recipes compose them. The author-facing primitives in the simulation tool.

pages.title: Sim execution model
pages.purpose: How the sim engine runs scenarios. Dispatcher, executor lifecycle, result aggregation, exit semantics.

pages.title: Stages and catalog (graph)
pages.purpose: How `graph` builds its picture of the codebase — the six-stage pipeline, the catalog format, and the content-keyed cache.

pages.title: Rules and gating (graph)
pages.purpose: The five graph rules, what each one detects, and how the save/compare gate flow integrates with CI.

pages.title: Adding a language to graph
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for writing a new GraphLanguageAdapter — Go, Java, C/C++, or anything else — without touching the engine.

pages.title: CLI dispatch
pages.purpose: How argv becomes a Tool action handler. The CLI bootstrap, registration order, the global flag set, error suggestions.

pages.title: Plugin loader
pages.purpose: How plugins are discovered, loaded, and registered. Source files, npm packages, project pinning, the sync command.

pages.title: Session and persistence
pages.purpose: What gets written to disk during and after a run. The runtime dir layout, session records, logs, reports, the cache.

pages.title: Language adapters (fitness)
pages.purpose: What the fitness LanguageAdapter is, the six bundled adapters, and how to author a new one.

pages.title: Check pack architecture
pages.purpose: How check packs are structured, the bundled six, scope filters, parameterization, and the marketplace shape.

pages.title: Architecture gate
pages.purpose: The baseline-and-compare workflow. Identity hash, line-shift invariance, partial-SARIF tolerance, CI integration patterns.

pages.title: CLI command tree
pages.purpose: Lookup-shaped reference for every CLI command, its flags, and when to use each.

pages.title: Plugin authoring
pages.purpose: Write your own check, recipe, scenario, check pack, or full Tool plugin. End-to-end.

pages.title: Dashboard
pages.purpose: The HTML report — what it shows, when it opens, how it's generated, and where it lives.

pages.title: Package catalog
pages.purpose: Flat reference of every package in the monorepo: name, path, layer, one-line role, key exports. Lookup-only; the conceptual layer narrative lives in 10-mental-model/03-modular-monolith.md.

pages.title: Configuration
pages.purpose: The opensip-tools.config.yml schema, every field, defaults, and where each is read.

pages.title: JSON output schema
pages.purpose: The CliOutput shape (and the SimDoneResult shape). Every field, every type, every presence rule.

pages.title: Coding standards
pages.purpose: How code in this workspace is written. ESLint posture, error handling, exit codes, log style.

pages.title: Layer policy
pages.purpose: The dependency-cruiser rules that enforce the five-layer architecture and the tool-internal partitioning rules (graph stages, dashboard panels), rule by rule, with rationale.

pages.title: Doc conventions
pages.purpose: Voice, frontmatter, diagrams, and verification trail conventions for the architecture doc set.
